title: Misleading search command in documentation's introductory section
body:
### Issue Description

The docs' 'Introduction' section (both for [stable](https://docs.podman.io/en/stable/Introduction.html) and [latest](https://docs.podman.io/en/latest/Introduction.html)) currently reads:

> Here’s how to find your first [Container Image](https://developers.redhat.com/blog/2018/02/22/container-terminology-practical-introduction/#h.dqlu6589ootw):
>
> ```
> podman search busybox
> ```
>
> Output:
> 
> > INDEX       NAME                                DESCRIPTION                                       STARS   OFFICIAL   AUTOMATED
> > docker.io   docker.io/library/busybox           Busybox base image.                               1882    [OK]
> > docker.io   docker.io/radial/busyboxplus        Full-chain, Internet enabled, busybox made f...   30                 [OK]
> > docker.io   docker.io/yauritux/busybox-curl     Busybox with CURL                                 8
> > ...

This is slightly misleading (at least under `Debian`, but if I get [the 'Configuration files' section of the installation guide](https://podman.io/docs/installation#configuration-files) right this also applies e.g. to `Fedora`), as no `unqualified-search-registries` is configured by default and in consequence issueing `podman search busybox` does not yield any result at all.

For a new user following along the 'Introduction' section, the `podman search busybox` may well be the very first command executed. The above mismatch results in a kind of bumpy onboarding experience, as one ends up searching the web for a solution pretty early.

Basically, the same applies to [the 'Getting started' section on podman.io](https://podman.io/docs#searching-pulling--listing-images), though to my understanding podman.io lives in [another GitHub repository](https://github.com/containers/podman.io).

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. Install podman
2. Issue `podman search busybox`
3. Get confused why (in constrast to the docs) there are no results for the search


### Describe the results you received

Empty string

### Describe the results you expected

A list of busybox images available on some default registries

### podman info output

```yaml
I guess this does not apply to the docs.
```


### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

None

### Upstream Latest Release

~~Yes~~ No

### Additional environment details

Debian

### Additional information

To improve the experience, I suggest to
- either guide new users through the process of configuring `unqualified-search-registries` or
- explicitly state a registry in the search command, e.g. `podman search docker.io/busybox`

comments.body:
Well c/image doesn't define any default registries, therefore I would assume most distros to ship the default file without adding registries.
I know arch does that: https://wiki.archlinux.org/title/Podman#No_image_found
I am also sure it is the same for debian.

@vrothberg I'm running Debian 11 which admittedly ships an _ancient_ version of podman (v3.0.1) (I noticed I ticked the wrong box in this regard when fileing this issue - I adapted the above description accordingly, sorry for this). So thanks to @Luap99 for pointing out that the issue remains for more recent versions.

Interesting that Debian doesn't set it.  @siretart is it intentional to leave the search registries blank in debian?  I'd expect it to be set to `docker.io` unless distributions decide to default to their distro-specific registry.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.

@vrothberg my intention is to followup upstream's guidance. So it should just follow the default upstream search registries. 

Maybe you could remind me where upstream sets the default search registries? I'm happy to verify the packaging.

@siretart upstream does not set any default.  The upstream registries.conf is more intended to make it easy to configure, see https://github.com/containers/image/blob/main/registries.conf.

@mtrmac, WDYT of adding Docker Hub to the upstream conf?

- (Nobody asked, but) I wouldn’t recommend a tutorial to start with `podman search`. You never know what it is going to find, and randomly running containers around on the internet is a bad idea. Trusting _any one_ company is better — so just type `docker.io/library/httpd` instead of searching for it.
- Given the risks of USR in image lookup, which we have worked _so hard_ to mitigate / avoid / make easy to use, I think it’s a bad idea to have `podman search` use that value, and I’d be unhappy about encouraging that. If we add `docker.io` _for `podman search`_, every distribution is going to want to add `registry.$distribution` to the list for `podman search` as well. I’d much rather have a `podman-search-default-registries` option somewhere.

After re-reading this bug report, the initial recommendation resonate most with me:

* guide new users through the process of configuring unqualified-search-registries 
* explicitly state a registry in the search command, e.g. podman search docker.io/busybox

I think both would be valuable additions to the docs/tutorials

> guide new users through the process of configuring unqualified-search-registries

For `podman pull` / `podman run`, I’d prefer that wouldn’t happen at all — it leads to scripts and `Containerfile`s which don’t work without USR set to particular values, and if two such files meet in a single system, they can conflict.
